source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: Mid-cap tech growth stocks are at or below 2018 trough multiples despite better fundamentals, creating a compelling entry point
summary_extended: Baker argues that mid-cap software and internet growth stocks have experienced a valuation round-trip back to 2018 trough multiples, despite materially better business fundamentals today than in 2018. He notes that in 2018, the 10-year Treasury was at 3% and the Fed was deep into a tightening cycle, yet current multiples are at or below those prior trough levels even though the companies are growing faster and generating better margins now. Baker states he is 'as bullish as I've ever been' on names that are down 60-70% from peak, and describes actively buying weakness in this cohort. The investment implication is a long position in beaten-down mid-cap software and internet names, with the thesis predicated on mean reversion of multiples toward levels justified by improved fundamentals. No specific company names or tickers were cited; the thesis is a broad sector-level view on the software and internet growth equity universe.
detail: Baker argues that software and internet multiples have round-tripped to 2018 levels, but the underlying businesses are growing faster and have better margins than in 2018. In 2018, the 10-year was at 3% and the market was deep into a tightening cycle. Today, companies are at or below those trough multiples with better fundamentals, making the opportunity set more attractive than it appears.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly states he is 'as bullish as I've ever been on these names that are down 60% to 70%' and describes buying weakness actively.
quote_fragment: as bullish as I've ever been on these names that are down 60% to 70%
